---
title: "Data Structures"
description: "Store, organize, search, and transform many values with the right container for the job."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [data-structures, beginner]
order: 130
---

## Why this chapter matters

Store, organize, search, and transform many values with the right container for the job.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.callout-note title="Guiding questions"}
- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Lists](lists.qmd) — Store, access, slice, update, copy, and loop over ordered mutable collections.
- [Tuples](tuples.qmd) — Use immutable ordered records and unpack their values clearly.
- [Dictionaries](dictionaries.qmd) — Use key-value mappings for lookup, looping, counting, grouping, and simple records.
- [Sets](sets.qmd) — Store unique values, test membership, and compare groups with set operations.
- [Nested Data](nested-data.qmd) — Represent structured information with containers inside containers.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "data-structures-overview",
  "title": "Data Structures overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Data Structures chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Store, organize, search, and transform many values with the right container for the job.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Store, organize, search, and transform many values with the right container for the job."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
